headline: North London Collegiate School to open first Hong Kong campus in 2026
dek: NLCS — ranked first in the UK for IB results in 2025 — is bringing its academic brand to Hong Kong, becoming the sixth school in the group's international network and the latest in a wave of new openings reshaping the city's school landscape.
author: marcus-chen

body.text: North London Collegiate School (NLCS), one of Britain's most academically selective independent schools, has announced plans to open a new campus in Hong Kong targeting a 2026 launch. The move would make NLCS Hong Kong the sixth school in the group's international network, which already includes campuses in Dubai, Singapore, Jeju and Japan.

The school is known for its consistently strong examination results. In 2025, NLCS (UK) was ranked first in the United Kingdom and second in the world for IB Diploma results, a record that gives its international brand considerable weight with families considering post-16 pathways in Asia.

Specific details — including campus location, fee schedule and admissions timeline — have not yet been publicly announced. Gwen Byrom, NLCS International's Director of Education Strategy, has said the team is excited to bring what she described as "NLCS's blend of academic ambition and individual support" to Hong Kong. Families interested in the school have been directed to register their interest while formal admissions processes are finalised.

The announcement places NLCS in the company of several other new arrivals to Hong Kong's international school sector this year. The YK Pao Education Foundation — which has longstanding educational operations in Shanghai — is also set to open a new bilingual school in Hong Kong in September 2026. Meanwhile, the English Schools Foundation (ESF), which runs 23 schools across the city, is adding three new kindergartens to its network, creating hundreds of additional early years places.
A busy year for Hong Kong school openings

Together with the sixth form expansions at Nord Anglia International School Hong Kong and Kellett School, the flurry of new campuses and programmes reflects a broader confidence in Hong Kong as an international education destination. Hong Kong's education secretary Christine Choi has said the city — with its highly internationalised academic environment — is ready to play an active role in global educational partnerships.

For expatriate families, the practical consequence is a significantly wider menu of British-aligned school options by the start of the 2026–27 academic year, spanning early years through to A Level and IB Diploma provision. School advisers note, however, that families considering NLCS Hong Kong should be prepared for further detail to emerge in the months ahead before making placement decisions.

Hong Kong has historically been one of Asia's most competitive international school markets, with demand from UHNW and senior expatriate families consistently outpacing supply at premium institutions. The current wave of openings, if it translates into full operational capacity, could ease waitlist pressure at some of the city's most sought-after British-curriculum schools.
